title: Creature Spec
description: The canonical, frozen consistency contract for the creature-swarm pipeline. The Field Interpreter agent reads the doodle once and emits exactly one Creature Spec; every downstream specialist keys off it so they all describe the SAME creature. Text agents (Biologist/Habitat/Society) mine the descriptive half (name, distinctive_features, vibe, and loose reference to parts/palette); structural agents (3D Modeler/Animator) treat body_plan, parts, palette, and locomotion as literal build parameters. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-creature-swarm-design.md, section 'Contract 1 — Creature Spec'.

name.description: Invented common name plus a mock-Latin binomial (e.g. 'Warbling Blorb (Blorbus canticus)'). Primary consumers: text agents (Biologist/Habitat/Society) and the coordinator's {{creature_name}} slot.

body_plan.description: The creature's overall structural bauplan. Primary consumers: structural agents (3D Modeler/Animator) as literal build parameters.

core_shape.description: The dominant primitive the body reads as. The 3D Modeler starts from this primitive when composing the mesh in trimesh.

symmetry.description: Body symmetry. Drives how the 3D Modeler mirrors/arranges parts and how the Animator distributes per-limb phase offsets.

size_est_m.description: Estimated longest-axis size in metres. Text agents use it for tone/scale; structural agents use it to scale the exported model.

parts.description: The creature's appendages and features as discrete, countable parts. Structural agents build one instance per count; text agents reference these loosely. May be empty for a featureless blob.

type.description: What the part is (e.g. 'leg', 'eyestalk', 'wing', 'antenna'). Free text so the Interpreter can name whatever the doodle shows.

count.description: How many of this part exist. The 3D Modeler instantiates exactly this many; the Animator phases exactly this many (relevant for locomotion).

shape.description: Free-text description of the part's form (e.g. 'tapering tube', 'flat paddle'). 3D Modeler maps it onto a primitive; text agents quote it.

placement.description: Where the part sits on the body (e.g. 'ventral, evenly spaced', 'dorsal midline'). Drives 3D placement and Animator gait grouping.

palette.description: The creature's colours as hex strings, most dominant first. Text agents cite colours descriptively; the 3D Modeler tints mesh parts from this list. At least one colour is required.

items.description: A CSS hex colour, 3- or 6-digit, leading '#'.

distinctive_features.description: Short prose callouts of what makes this creature memorable (e.g. 'forked tail', 'bioluminescent spots'). Primary consumers: text agents, which build narrative around them. At least one is required so every guide has a hook.

locomotion.description: How the creature moves. Text agents use it for behavioural flavour; the Animator uses it to pick the parametric walk-cycle model.

vibe.description: One-line personality read of the creature. Primary consumers: text agents for tone and the coordinator's {{tagline}} slot.
